sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of chayotes grown from Sechium edule (Jacq.) Sw., of the Cucurbitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Chayotes for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of chayotes grown from Sechium edule (Jacq.) Sw., of the Cucurbitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Chayotes for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of chayotes grown from Sechium edule (Jacq.) Sw., of the Cucurbitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Chayotes for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The chayotes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The chayotes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the chayotes must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Chayotes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=3
Chayotes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Chayotes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Chayotes in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may b e allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
­ slight defects in shape, i.e. lightly marked longitudinal grooves and slight depressions;
­ slight defects in colouring, not exceeding 25% of the total surface area;
­ slight skin defects due to scarring, not exceeding a total of 3 cm2.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes chayotes which do not qualify  for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the chayotes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, i.e. lightly marked longitudinal grooves and slight depressions; defects in colouring, not exceeding 35% of the total surface area; skin defects due to scarring, not exceeding a total of 5 cm2. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.
sections.retrieval_text:
2.2.3 CLASS II
number=2.2.3
level=1
This class includes chayotes which do not qualify  for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the chayotes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, i.e. lightly marked longitudinal grooves and slight depressions; defects in colouring, not exceeding 35% of the total surface area; skin defects due to scarring, not exceeding a total of 5 cm2. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

page_spans.text: This class includes chayotes which do not qualify  for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the chayotes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, i.e. lightly marked longitudinal grooves and slight depressions; defects in colouring, not exceeding 35% of the total surface area; skin defects due to scarring, not exceeding a total of 5 cm2. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by weight or by length, with a minimum weight of 200 g or a minimum length of 12 cm, in accordance with the following table: Size Code Weight (in grams) Length (cm) A 200 - 300 12 - 14 B 301 - 400 15 - 16 C 401 - 500 > 16 D > 500 The difference in weight between individual fruits under size code D may not exceed 150 g.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of chayotes corresponding to the size immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only chayotes of the same variety and/or commercial type, origin, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Chayotes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the chayotes. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CHA YOTES
pages.summary: Página inicial com título principal do documento e informações de padrão e datas de adoção/amendagem.
Página com o mapeamento hierárquico dos títulos e respectivo texto para os níveis 1, 2 e 3, incluindo marcação de continuidade na seção 2.2.2 e seus superiores.

Página contendo seções numeradas 2.2.3 a 5, com subseções detalhadas em 4.1 e 5, refletindo texto descritivo e tabelas para os requisitos de chayote. Marcação de continuidade na seção 2.2.3, indicando continuidade da página anterior.

A página apresenta a continuação da seção 5.2.1 e inicia a nova seção 6 MARKING OR LABELLING com subseções detalhadas de 6.1 e 6.2 e suas respectivas subsubseções, finalizando com as seções 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE. A hierarquia segue a numeração clara e os títulos em caixa alta são nível 1. A seção 5.2.1 está com is_continuation_from_previous_page=true, indicando sua continuidade da página anterior.
